evals.prompt: Run the replatforming observer against https://talkingslop.ai. Compare the source and generated site at desktop and mobile sizes, then make one evidence-backed improvement to replatform-site and rerun the benchmark.
evals.expected_output: A baseline/candidate visual and efficiency benchmark, paired browser evidence, a generalized DIF to replatform-site, and a keep or revert decision.
assertions: Captures paired original/generated screenshots at desktop and mobile viewports.
Records duration and records tokens/credits or an explicit unavailable reason.
Changes the inner replatform-site skill rather than only patching the generated site.
Writes a DIF outcome record and concrete diff before candidate validation.
Reruns the same benchmark and applies explicit regression gates.

evals.prompt: Observe replatform-site across a Webflow site and a WordPress site in parallel. Find recurring visual or behavioral migration failures, improve the inner skill, and report quality-versus-cost tradeoffs.
evals.expected_output: Isolated parallel site runs, a cross-site failure taxonomy, aggregate metrics with coverage, and a generalized verified inner-skill improvement.
assertions: Uses isolated computer-use-capable runs for independent sites.
Distinguishes site-specific mismatches from recurring provider or general workflow failures.
Aggregates quality, latency, tokens, and credits without inventing unavailable telemetry.
Every completed site run emits a concrete proposed diff or an explicit no-change DIF outcome.
Keeps only a candidate that passes controlled baseline-versus-candidate gates.

evals.prompt: Benchmark the efficiency of replatform-site over repeated runs of the same public site. Look for duplicated work and token-heavy steps, make the smallest general improvement, and verify visual quality does not regress.
evals.expected_output: A controlled repeated-run efficiency benchmark and a minimal verified DIF that reduces waste without reducing visual parity.
assertions: Keeps model, harness, site, routes, viewports, and cache policy comparable.
Identifies repeated work from evidence rather than only aggregate totals.
Reports metric coverage and variance across repeated runs.
Rejects efficiency improvements that cause visual or correctness regressions.
